description:
Consumer report from trip-planner project. CRITICAL.

Problem: The TypeScript type definitions for hx-tabs are wrong. helix.d.ts declares:
- active?: string
- placement?: string  
- size?: string

But the actual CEM shows:
- orientation (not placement)
- activation (not active)
- label

The event detail type is also wrong: declares { active: string } but actual is { tabId: string, index: number }.

This causes type errors in consumer projects and misleading autocomplete suggestions.

Fix: Regenerate helix.d.ts from the current CEM, or add a CI gate that validates type definitions against CEM on every release.

Acceptance criteria:
- HxTabsAttributes matches actual CEM properties exactly
- Tab change event detail type matches actual payload
- All other component type defs verified against CEM
title: fix: helix.d.ts HxTabsAttributes properties drift from CEM
